questions.question: In Android, an Intent Filter is used to specify the components that can receive an Intent.
options: A) To send data to another app
B) To specify the components that can receive an Intent
C) To start a new Activity
D) To broadcast a message
questions.explanation: An Intent Filter is used to specify the components that can receive an Intent, allowing them to filter out unwanted Intents.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the AndroidManifest.xml file in relation to Intent Filters?
options: A) To declare the name of the app
B) To specify the components that can receive an Intent
C) To define the app's permissions
D) To declare the app's version code
questions.explanation: The AndroidManifest.xml file is used to declare the Intent Filters for each component in the app, allowing them to receive and handle Intents.

questions.question: When does an Activity's Intent Filter match an Intent?
options: A) When the Intent is broadcast
B) When the Intent is received by the Activity
C) When the Activity is started
D) When the Intent is sent to another app
questions.explanation: An Activity's Intent Filter matches an Intent when the Activity is started, allowing it to receive and handle the Intent.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the onCreate() method in an Activity's lifecycle?
options: A) To start the Activity
B) To resume the Activity
C) To pause the Activity
D) To initialize the Activity's state
questions.explanation: The onCreate() method is called when an Activity is created and is used to initialize the Activity's state, including its UI and any necessary data.

questions.question: When does a Broadcast Receiver's Intent Filter match an Intent?
options: A) When the Intent is broadcast
B) When the Intent is received by the Broadcast Receiver
C) When the Intent is installed on the device
D) When the Intent is deleted
questions.explanation: A Broadcast Receiver's Intent Filter matches an Intent when the Intent is broadcast, allowing the Receiver to receive and handle the Intent.
